apply: Apply the concepts of motion in a plane to solve problems involving velocity, acceleration, and displacement.
Use the equations of motion in a plane to determine the position, velocity, and acceleration of an object at a given time.
analyze: Analyze the effect of friction on the motion of an object in a plane.
Compare the motion of an object in a plane with and without air resistance.
remember: Recall the concept of motion in a plane and its importance in physics.
Mention the types of motion in a plane (rectilinear and curved motion).
understand: Explain the difference between rectilinear and curved motion.
Describe the conditions for uniform and non-uniform motion in a plane.
Understand the concept of acceleration in a plane and its relation to velocity.

ncert_objectives: Define the motion of a particle in two dimensions and the conditions under which it is possible.
Distinguish between uniform and non-uniform motion in a plane.
Explain the concept of acceleration in a plane and its relation to velocity.
Derive the equations of motion in a plane.
board_exam_objectives: Define motion in a plane and explain the conditions for rectilinear and curved motion.
Derive and apply the equations of motion in a plane to solve problems.
Explain the concept of acceleration in a plane and its relation to velocity.
Analyze the effect of friction and air resistance on the motion of an object in a plane.
